SUMMARY:
@AvinashAbbigeri **Looks good to merge 👍.**
**Thanks for contributing 🚀 🚀.**
You may join our _Discord server_ - **https://discord.gg/DeT4jXPfkG** to get updates about the project.
v2:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds a legal-use disclaimer to the README and website with a new `/disclaimer` page. The implementation follows the same structural and styling patterns as existing legal pages (terms, privacy-policy), making it a straightforward addition. However, the new page lacks error handling for its external fetch, the PR description contains a misleading statement about the content source URL, and there's a missing trailing newline in the text file.

POINTS:
- [Website/app/disclaimer/page.tsx:2-3] No error handling around the `fetch()` call. If the GitHub raw URL is unreachable during Vercel build (network outage, rate limit, 404), the page will crash with an unhandled error. Other pages in the codebase handle this properly — `docs/layout.jsx` uses try-catch with a null fallback, and `download/page.tsx` catches errors and displays a user-friendly message. At minimum, wrap the fetch in try-catch. Note: the existing `terms/page.tsx` and `privacy-policy/page.tsx` also lack error handling — worth addressing in a follow-up.
- [Website/app/disclaimer/page.tsx:2-3] PR description states "Disclaimer text is currently loaded from my fork" but the URL `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty/master/disclaimer.txt` points to the **main repo** (`SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty`), not a fork. Clarify the description or correct the URL if a fork was intended.
- [Website/app/disclaimer/page.tsx:2] No cache option specified on the `fetch()` call. In Next.js server components, fetch caching defaults vary by version. Add `{ cache: "force-cache" }` to make caching intent explicit and prevent unexpected re-fetches during builds.
- [disclaimer.txt:8] Missing trailing newline at end of file (diff shows `\ No newline at end of file`). Standard POSIX convention — add a final newline.
- [Website/components/Footer.tsx:16] Changed `flex-1` to `flex-auto` and `space-x-6` to `space-x-4` on the navigation `<ul>`. `flex-1` (`flex: 1 1 0%`) and `flex-auto` (`flex: 1 1 auto`) behave differently with content sizing. Confirm this was intentional to accommodate the new disclaimer link rather than an accidental modification.
v3:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds a legal-use disclaimer to the README and creates a new `/disclaimer` page on the website, linked from the footer. The implementation mirrors the existing pattern used by privacy-policy and terms pages (fetching raw content from GitHub), but introduces no error handling and bundles unrelated dependency updates.

POINTS:
- [Website/app/disclaimer/page.tsx:3-5] No error handling on the `fetch()` call. If the network fails, the URL returns 404, or the response is malformed, the page will crash with an unhandled promise rejection. The existing `Website/app/docs/layout.jsx` demonstrates a better pattern: wrap fetch in try-catch and return a fallback. Consider adopting that same approach here (and ideally in privacy-policy/terms as a follow-up).

- [Website/app/disclaimer/page.tsx:4] The PR description states: "Disclaimer text is currently loaded from my fork. Once this PR is merged, we can update the link to point to the main repo." However, the URL in the code already points to `SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty/master/disclaimer.txt`, which IS the main repo. Clarify this discrepancy — either the description is outdated or the URL needs to be changed.

- [disclaimer.txt:8] Missing trailing newline. The diff shows `\ No newline at end of file`. Most text files should end with a newline character for POSIX compliance and to avoid potential issues with file concatenation tools.

- [Website/package-lock.json] This PR includes `@eslint/plugin-kit` and `@eslint/core` version bumps (0.3.1→0.3.4, 0.14.0→0.15.1) that are unrelated to the disclaimer feature. Dependency updates should be in a separate PR to keep change history clean and simplify revertability.

- [Website/components/Footer.tsx:16] The `space-x-6` was changed to `space-x-4` on the navigation list, but this style adjustment is not mentioned in the PR description. If intentional, add it to the changelog; if accidental, revert it to preserve the original spacing.

- [Website/app/disclaimer/page.tsx:1] The page is an `async` Server Component with no `export const revalidate` or caching directive. Since this fetches external content at build time with no cache configuration, it will re-fetch on every build. Consider adding `export const revalidate = 3600;` (or similar) to avoid unnecessary network calls and reduce build fragility.
v4:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds a legal-use disclaimer to the README and website, following the existing pattern established by the terms and privacy-policy pages. The implementation is consistent with the codebase conventions but has a few areas for improvement.

POINTS:
- [Website/app/disclaimer/page.tsx:2-4] No error handling for the `fetch()` call — if the GitHub raw URL is unreachable (network error, 404, rate limit), the server component will throw an unhandled error with no fallback UI. The existing `terms` and `privacy-policy` pages have the same issue, but the client-side `download` page demonstrates a better pattern with try/catch, loading state, and a retry button. Consider adding at minimum a `try/catch` that returns a graceful fallback message.

- [Website/app/disclaimer/page.tsx:3] The URL `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty/master/disclaimer.txt` points to the `master` branch, which is mutable. The `privacy-policy` page uses a pinned commit hash (`c86ce857...`) for stability. Consider pinning to a commit hash after merge for consistency and to prevent the page from breaking if the file is renamed/moved.

- [disclaimer.txt:8] Missing newline at end of file (shown as `\ No newline at end of file` in the diff). Add a trailing newline to comply with POSIX conventions and avoid potential diff noise in future edits.

- [README.md:89-94 vs disclaimer.txt:1-8] The disclaimer text in README.md and disclaimer.txt are duplicated but not identical in formatting. If the content diverges in the future, users will see different disclaimers depending on where they look. Consider either: (a) sourcing the README section from a shared file, or (b) adding a comment noting these should stay in sync.

- [Website/package-lock.json] This PR includes unrelated `@eslint/plugin-kit` dependency updates (0.3.1 → 0.3.4 with nested `@eslint/core` resolution). These lockfile changes are not related to the disclaimer feature and should be in a separate PR to keep the change history clean and simplify rollback if needed.

- [Website/components/Footer.tsx:16] The footer's `<ul>` class changed from `flex-1 space-x-6` to `flex-auto space-x-4`. This is a behavioral change (different flex grow behavior and tighter spacing) that's not mentioned in the PR description. Verify this was intentional and doesn't cause layout issues on narrow screens with the additional disclaimer link.
